# REGIME READ — 2026-06-18 (intraday update)

## Changes Since Previous Read
- **Stage: 3 → 3 (unchanged).** Still neutral/choppy. Stage 2 promotion requires more than just VIX relief; Stage 4 demotion still requires a 50DMA close-break.
- **Total score: -1 → 0 (delta +1).** Dealer Flow improves -1 → 0 as VIX crushes -4.99% intraday to 17.52 (post-event vol-unwind beginning). All other lenses hold.
- **Triggers from previous read:**
  - **Bullish — TQQQ daily close back above $80.13:** **ARMED, not yet tested.** Jun 18 close pending; pre-open setup will determine.
  - **Bullish — VXN crush through 22:** **NOT FIRED.** VXN unchanged at 28.56 in snapshot. The VIX is crushing (17.52, -4.99%) but the VXN gap to VIX is now ~11 points — even WIDER than yesterday's ~10 points. NDX-specific tail risk pricing has NOT relented.
  - **Bullish — Clean MOU signing Jun 19 + benign Philly Fed today:** **STILL ARMED.** Philly Fed prints 8:30 ET today (forecast +11.4 vs prior -0.4); MOU signing Jun 19.
  - **Bullish — F&G break above 45:** **ARMED, not yet measured Jun 18.** Previous 39.5.
  - **Bearish — TQQQ daily close below 50DMA $76.39:** **ARMED, ON THE EDGE.** Estimated spot still ~$77.7 → +1.7% cushion. Today's price action decides.
  - **Bearish — VIX sustained above 22:** **CLEARED for now.** VIX 17.52 -4.99% intraday — moving AWAY from trigger.
  - **Bearish — HY OAS through 300 bp:** **NOT FIRED.** Last read 278 bp; no fresh print indicated.
  - **Bearish — Israel re-engagement pre-Geneva:** **NOT FIRED.** No headlines indicated; MOU track intact.
  - **Bearish — Trump tariff escalation:** **NOT FIRED Jun 18 intraday.**
- **Notable lens flips:** Dealer Flow -1 → 0 (VIX relief). All other lenses held.

## Regime Stage Tracker
- **Current Stage: 3 — Neutral/choppy** (Stage 4 trigger still ARMED at the 50DMA gate; Stage 2 promotion ARMED if vol-crush extends and Jun 19 binaries land cleanly).
- **Stage Anchor:** Jun 17, 2026 — Warsh's hawkish FOMC.
- **Position:** Day 2 of Stage 3. Three binaries inside the 5-day window: Philly Fed + Jobless Claims Jun 18 (today 8:30 ET), MOU formal signing Jun 19, June Quad-Witching Jun 19.
- **Confidence: Medium.** Dealer Flow relief is real but VXN gap to VIX widened; Macro headwind unchanged; geopolitical + credit cushion intact. Tape direction is the deciding factor next 48 hours.
- **Bullish trigger (→ Stage 2):** TQQQ daily close above $80.13 AND VXN crushes through 22 AND clean MOU signing Jun 19 AND benign Philly Fed (not so hot it reinforces hawkish narrative) AND no follow-on hawkish Fed-speak in next 5 sessions.
- **Bearish trigger (→ Stage 4):** TQQQ daily close below 50DMA $76.39, OR VIX sustains above 22 for 2+ sessions, OR HY OAS through 300 bp, OR hot Philly Fed + tight jobless claims combo today, OR MOU collapse / Israel re-engagement pre-Geneva, OR Trump tariff escalation.

## Regime Verdict
US risk environment **remains Stage 3 neutral-choppy with first signs of post-FOMC vol relief (VIX -4.99%) but VXN gap widening to ~11 points keeps NDX-specific tail pricing elevated**; Medium confidence; macro headwind unchanged, credit and geopolitics still cushion downside, TQQQ still one ordinary down day from breaking the 50DMA gate.

SPX vol is releasing, NDX vol is NOT. SKEW/VVIX/term structure not retrievable |

HY OAS 278 bp last read (still tight) |

| **TOTAL** | **0** | — | Net neutral — Dealer Flow relief offsetting Macro/Sentiment headwind; Cross-Asset + Geopolitical cushion intact |

## Diverging Signals
- **VIX-VXN gap WIDENED Jun 18 (~11 pts vs ~10 pts Jun 17).** SPX vol is releasing on FOMC clarity; NDX vol is not. Dealers are still pricing leveraged-tech tail risk while broader equity tail risk relaxes. For a TQQQ put-seller, this means premium richness persists even as SPX vol normalises — but the asymmetry (NDX vol > SPX vol) is widening, not narrowing.
- **Cross-asset rotation INTO USD (DXY +0.68% reclaiming 100.77), OUT OF Gold (-2.26%), Brent soft (-1.33%).** Classic hawkish-Fed-with-Iran-relief signature: dollar firms on rate-path repricing, gold loses the geopolitical-tail bid, oil continues digesting the relief premium. Confirms macro headwind is the dominant near-term driver, not crisis re-pricing.
- **10Y up to 4.487 (+0.54% intraday Jun 18) while VIX falls -4.99%.** Bond market is reinforcing the hawkish-Fed signal while equity vol relaxes. The two signals are about DIFFERENT things: bond market about policy path (sticky), equity vol about event clarity (releasing). The bond signal is more durable.
- **F&G last read 39.5 going into vol relief without sentiment update.** If Jun 18 sentiment lifts despite the hawkish anchor, that's a tape-led rebound; if it stays sub-40, sentiment is reading the macro signal correctly and the equity vol relief is technical not fundamental.

## TQQQ Read
- **Momentum:** Last reliable read RSI ~60-65 post-Jun 17 close (cooled from 69.04 intraday peak). Two-day down streak Jun 16-17. Jun 18 outcome decides: a green day cools the streak and resets momentum to neutral; another red day extends streak and tightens the oversold setup.
- **Market structure: SIDEWAYS (held).** -11.8% from 4-wk high $88.09 (above -20% DOWNTURN threshold) AND +14.1% above 4-wk low $68.08 (below +30% RALLY threshold). DOWNTURN activation still needs close ≤ $70.47 + VXN ≥ 25; VXN condition already met at 28.56, so a ~9% further drop triggers DOWNTURN. RALLY activation needs close ≥ $88.50.
- **Trend:** Price still above both 50DMA ($76.39) and 200DMA ($68.09). **50DMA cushion ~+1.7% — one ordinary down day from breaking.** 200DMA cushion +14.1% (real structural support).
- **Volatility & premium environment:** **VXN held at 28.56 while VIX fell -4.99%.** Two takeaways: (1) NDX-specific tail-risk pricing did NOT release with the broader vol unwind — dealers still pricing leverage-tech path risk; (2) TQQQ premium richness persists, but the persistence reflects realized-risk repricing, not event-window overhang. Expected move ±12.1%; one-sigma downside $68.3 retests 4-wk low / 200DMA confluence.
- **Put-selling context: CAUTIOUS (held).** Five reasons unchanged: (1) **Macro lens decisively bearish** — DXY reclaiming 100.77 and 10Y 4.487 confirm hawkish-Fed follow-through. (2) **TQQQ still +1.7% above 50DMA $76.39** — gate intact but cushion compressed; Jun 18 close decides. (3) **VXN 28.56 unchanged + EM ±12.1%** — premium rich but range wide; one-sigma down retests $68 support confluence. (4) **Quad-witching + MOU signing stack Jun 19** — pin-unwind + delta-hedging + geopolitical execution converge. (5) **Sentiment last read F&G 39.5** with no fresh data. *Counter:* VIX -4.99% Jun 18 is the first sign of post-FOMC vol normalization; credit holding (HY OAS 278 last read); Iran MOU on track; 200DMA cushion +14.1%. **Net: CAUTIOUS — VIX relief is a tentative positive but not enough to lift to FAVOURABLE given VXN persistence + the 50DMA edge + Macro lens unchanged.** A clean Jun 18 close + benign Philly Fed + Jun 19 MOU signing without surprise could swing the read; conversely, a 50DMA break flips to HOSTILE. Context only — no strikes, sizes, expiries, or trade calls.

## Forward Levels & Triggers
- **Bullish trigger (→ Stage 2):** TQQQ daily close back above $80.13 AND VXN crushes through 22 (still ~6.5 points to go) AND benign Philly Fed Jun 18 + clean MOU signing Jun 19 AND no follow-on hawkish Fed-speak in next 5 sessions AND F&G reclaims 45.
- **Bearish trigger (→ Stage 4):** TQQQ daily close below 50DMA $76.39 (only -1.7% from estimated spot), OR VIX re-spikes and sustains above 22 for 2+ sessions, OR HY OAS through 300 bp, OR hot Philly Fed + tight jobless claims reinforcing hawkish tape, OR MOU collapse / Israel re-engagement pre-Geneva, OR Trump tariff escalation.
- **Neutralizer:** A clean week with no incremental hawkish Fed-speak (Warsh now skips inter-meeting pressers — structurally fewer comm events), benign Philly Fed today, clean MOU Friday, and a relief-bid tech mega-cap print (Accenture today, Micron late June, mega-caps late July) that re-establishes growth leadership.

## This Period's Catalysts
- **Jun 18, 2026 (TODAY):** Philly Fed Manufacturing 8:30 ET (forecast +11.4 vs prior -0.4); Initial Jobless Claims 8:30 ET; Accenture (ACN), Kroger (KR) earnings.
- **Jun 19, 2026 (Fri):** US-Iran MOU formal Geneva signing (VP Vance + Speaker Ghalibaf); June Quad-Witching Expiry.
- **Jun 22, 2026 (Mon):** First post-quad / post-MOU tape day.
- **Late June: Micron (MU) earnings** — first major Q2-tape semis read.
- **Jun 30, 2026 (Tue):** Quarter-end rebalance.
- **Jul 3, 2026 (Fri):** NFP June payrolls.

## Expiry-Window Flag
- Catalysts inside the next 5 trading days (Jun 18 today, Jun 19, Jun 22, Jun 23, Jun 24): Philly Fed + Initial Claims + ACN/KR earnings Jun 18; MOU Geneva signing + June Quad-Witching Jun 19; first post-event tape Jun 22.
- **Window: EVENT-WINDOW** — geopolitical-execution + structural expiry within 2 sessions; data prints today; FOMC volatility still digesting.

## Structural Calendar Flag (deterministic — always report)
- **Quad-witching:** Next is **Jun 19, 2026** (3rd Friday). Days-until: **1 trading day.** **WITHIN 2 SESSIONS.** Elevated volume + pinning + delta-hedge unwind dominate; stacks with MOU signing.
- **Quarter-end rebalance:** Next is **Jun 30, 2026** (last business day of Q2). Days-until: **~8 trading days.** Outside 2-session window but inside broader expiry-rebalance complex.
- **Net:** **Jun 19 remains structurally exceptional** — quad-witching + MOU formal signing + post-FOMC week-close converge in one session. Intraday vol/volume structurally elevated; pinning unusual; short-put fills behave differently than typical Friday. Descriptive only — no trade calls.

## EVENT ALERT — Historical Playbook

- **Philadelphia Fed Manufacturing — Jun 18, 2026 8:30 ET (forecast +11.4 vs prior -0.4, post-hawkish-FOMC tape).** Data print, not decision event, but in a freshly-hawkish regime carries asymmetric reflexivity.
  - **Philly Fed Jul 17, 2008 (regional-deterioration regime):** Print missed badly (-16.3 vs -8.0 expected); SPX -1.1% on day, accelerated existing weakness. Lesson: in a regime already pricing tight policy, big data misses one-way down.
  - **Philly Fed Apr 18, 2024 (hot-inflation regime):** Beat headline (+15.5 vs +1.5 expected); 10Y +5bp on day, SPX -0.22%, growth-tech underperformed. Lesson: in a hot-inflation hawkish-Fed regime, hot beats reinforce tighter-for-longer and hurt leveraged growth specifically.
  - **Net alert:** Meaningful Philly Fed beat today (≥ +14) reinforces hawkish FOMC tape and pressures rate-sensitive growth (TQQQ-relevant). Meaningful miss (<+5) gives Fed cover to soften posture and could anchor relief bounce. Asymmetry leans negative — hawkish-Fed already locked in; hot data confirms; cool data partially relieves.

- **US-Iran MOU formal Geneva signing — Jun 19, 2026 (combined with June quad-witching).** Historical signing-day anchors: **Camp David Accords Sep 1978** (SPX +0.7%, framework already priced); **Israel-Egypt Treaty Mar 1979** (markets flat); **JCPOA Jul 14, 2015** (SPX +0.1%, oil -2.6% — framework already priced). Pattern: **formal signing rarely catalyst when framework already public.** Combined with quad-witching: pin-unwind + delta hedging amplify any directional move. Brent already digested most relief premium ($78.49 from $90+ pre-MOU). Residual tails: (a) Lebanon clause; (b) Iran walk-back on specific term; (c) Trump escalation pre-ceremony.

- **Net alert:** Carrying short-put exposure on TQQQ through this 5-session window means accepting (1) hawkish-Fed macro headwind structurally in price path (median 2026 dot 3.8%, 9-of-18 hike posture, DXY reclaiming par, 10Y firming), (2) thin 50DMA cushion ~+1.7% — single ordinary down day breaks it and flips read to HOSTILE, (3) Jun 19 quad-witching + MOU signing structural elevation, (4) VXN sticky at 28.56 (no NDX-specific crush) with EM lower bound $68.3 testing 4-wk-low / 200DMA confluence in a one-sigma move, (5) Philly Fed asymmetry leaning negative. Cushion items: VIX -4.99% Jun 18 (first event-unwind sign), credit tight, MOU executing tomorrow, 200DMA still +14.1% below spot. Historical hawkish-FOMC-into-event-week episodes (Jun 2022, Sep 2022) saw 3-5% follow-through downside the following week. **Descriptive only — no trade calls.**

> **Hard-rules compliance notes:**
> - Analysis only. No structures, strikes, expiries, or sizing anywhere.
> - **Jun 18 is intraday-update mode** — equity index closes are still Jun 17 in the live snapshot block; VIX/DXY/Brent/Gold/10Y are live Jun 18. VXN still showing Jun 17 (no Jun 18 quote in snapshot).
> - TQQQ Jun 17 close still not retrievable from search; ~$77.7 marked clearly as estimate from NDX -0.99% (3x approximation). Jun 18 intraday TQQQ not retrievable.
> - VIX 17.52, VXN 28.56, DXY 100.77, Brent 78.49, Gold 4,260.30, 10Y 4.487 from live snapshot block — used verbatim.
> - **Key Jun 18 update is the VXN-VIX gap WIDENING (~11 pts vs ~10 pts) despite VIX crush** — the NDX-specific premium persists, which materially shapes the TQQQ read.
> - Philly Fed Jun 18 actual print not yet available; treated as forward catalyst with playbook for beat/miss.
> - Conservative scoring: Dealer Flow upgraded only -1 → 0 (not 0 → +1) because VXN did NOT crush; Macro held at -2 because rate-path repricing is sticky (10Y/DXY firming Jun 18 confirms); Sentiment held at -1 absent Jun 18 F&G update.
> - Stage 3 held (not promoted to 2) because Stage 2 promotion requires VXN crush + tape confirmation; Confidence Medium because 50DMA gate still one ordinary down day away.
